name: Warm Terrine of Sausage, Peppers, Polenta And
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb sweet sausage baked for 20 minutes at 350 degrees drained and crumbled
1 large red bell pepper, cored, seeded into 1/2-inch wide strips
1 large green bell pepper, cored, seeded into 1/2-inch wide strips
1/2 lb fresh mozzarella, cut into 3 inch by 1/4-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Crumble the cooked sausage to resemble rough bread crumbs. Set aside.
2. In a medium saute pan, heat olive oil. Gently saute garlic cloves until golden brown on all sides, about 8 to 10 minutes. Add peppers and saute until soft, but not browned, about 7 to 8 minutes longer. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. Bring mozzarella to room temperature. Place sausage, peppers, garlic, and mozzarella in separate bowls.
4. Boil 6 cups water with salt and sugar. Slowly add cornmeal to boiling water in a thin stream, whisking continuously. Lower heat and cook until polenta resembles hot cereal. Remove from heat.
5. Pour polenta into terrine mold about 3/4-inch from the top. Sprinkle crumbled sausage over polenta. Cover with about 1-1/2 cups warm polenta. Flatten and smooth.
6. Layer peppers and garlic cloves, then another layer of warm polenta (1-1/2 cups). Smooth and flatten to create a flat surface to the edges. Place mozzarella over polenta without bringing it to edges. Pour remaining warm polenta to fill terrine. Smooth top layer to edges.
7. Chill terrine overnight.
8. To serve, invert terrine over cutting board. Slice as bread, about 3/4-inch thick. Place slice on baking sheet and bake for 10 to 12 minutes. Sprinkle with grated cheese and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 appetizers
